reviews.review_description:
After running for a while, I wanna puke because I feel uncomfortable while running in a virtual setting.

Update: thank you very much for letting me know and for your effort in changing this situation. I tried today running again, but still felt very dizzy. 😵‍💫 maybe I think running on a treadmill is a much better choice for me;)

developer_response.body:
Thank you for trying it again and for the kind feedback! I totally understand — everyone's body reacts differently to VR, and your comfort comes first. Whatever gets you moving and feeling good is the right choice, treadmill or otherwise. 
Wishing you happy running! 🏃

reviews.review_description: I really like this game- it’s fun running around collecting coins and completing challenges. I only gave 4 stars because there’s not much new content ever added

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your support!
I will try to make some time to add some new content in the future :)

reviews.review_description: It would be so great if you and conquers challenge could do a collaboration. Perhaps you mapping one of their virtual locations. They already have licensing. A win for everyone!

developer_response.body:
Hi!
Thanks for your feedback!
I will look into that :)

Have a great day!

reviews.review_title: A regular part of my daily workout!
reviews.review_description:
This is getting very close to what I've been looking for since running on a Wii board!! Lol It is constantly growing and changing, and I anxiously await every update. I'm currently trying to save up as much energy as I can to chase those rabbits for more than 3 levels!!

I love the Easter game!! The rabbits are so cute!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your constant feedback!

reviews.review_description:
I like the concept! 
Using it with my elliptical device. Would love to see a model that turning the head is just for head movement and not turning the direction of the advancement. 🙏

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback and for trying out my VR running app with your elliptical device! 😊 I'm glad to hear you like the concept.

I understand your suggestion about head movement and direction control. Currently, you can lock the direction by pressing a button on the hand controller. Additionally, I have created three levels where the direction is set in 1D, which works great with elliptical machines.

I did test a feature where the player follows a predefined path, but found that automatic turns could cause dizziness, so I haven't released it yet. Your input is valuable, and I'll continue to work on making the game more enjoyable and comfortable for everyone.

Thanks again for your support and patience! 🙏

reviews.review_description:
I asked for the meta quest 3 for
 Christmas just so I could "run" with the game.  You can't gain any momentum to think about burning calories.  Is it too late to ask for a refund?

developer_response.body:
Hi there!

First of all, I'm truly flattered that you got the Meta Quest 3 just to play my game! 😊 While the game might not provide the momentum you're looking for to burn calories through running, you can still get a great workout by incorporating some good squats or using the game on a cross trainer. You can even adjust the sensitivity in the settings to force you to work harder to get into speed.

Regarding a refund for the glasses, you'll need to contact Meta directly, as I'm just a single developer creating a free game on the Quest store to help people get active in a fun way.

Thank you for your support and understanding!

reviews.review_description: This is almost what I am looking for. I would love to find a VR game like the old Wii “Walk it Out” game. I think there is a calling for it due to the number of posts I’ve seen looking for walking apps like that one. Great job and looking forward to updates!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your kind words and thoughtful feedback! I'm thrilled to hear that you find my VR running app interesting and unique. 
While I haven't tried the Wii game you mentioned, I strive to make my app as good as possible. It's great to know that there's a demand for walking apps similar to it. Your suggestion is invaluable, and I've just added more sensitivity modes so you can adjust the speed to your own movement. The update was released just a few minutes ago! 
Stay tuned for more exciting features, and thank you for your support!

reviews.review_description: I thought it would be a workout but it's standing still.  I'm new to VR so ... there is that

developer_response.body:
Hi! I am sorry to hear that. 
Did you follow the tutorial? Do some deep squats and I think you will get a good result 😊

reviews.review_description:
update:
This app is interesting but technically poor. the low poly style was made as an obligatory choice, because certainly with many polygons the framerate would not hold up. 

and even despite the low poly there is a fairly persistent fog and the mountains in the distance can be seen appearing and disappearing, so the viewing area is restricted to a limited circle. yet we have lowpoly! imagine what would have happened with realistic graphics! I practically couldn't see more than 10 meters away! 

But you can see that there is a commitment and passion from the developers and that they strongly believe in this app, and I'm only pleased and the results are visible.

I would like to ask you to increase the graphic performance, while remaining in the low poly style, increase the number of objects in the scene, increase the viewing distance up to the entire horizon. 

but I believe this is not currently possible otherwise you would have already done it. 

I have known and used the Happy run app for a long time, which has superb graphics and a viewing distance that reaches across the entire horizon, it's incredible! 

unfortunately the developer of that app is a bit too slow and has been behind it for years now but for him it is just a hobby to which he evidently dedicates little time. 

I would have liked to buy some of your levels but I'm not sure what I'll find, given what's in the free levels I don't think the paid levels can be better. 

From the way I see the app now the rating I would like to give is 3 stars, but this happens because I know Happyrun and make comparisons, if I didn't know it I would give 4 stars. 

I will still give four stars because you can see that there is passion and you believe in the app 

a tips:
-you just have to improve technically. clearly staying in the lowpoly style but please at least increase the viewing distance and add more props.

-------------------------------------------



I appreciate the effort of you developers in having created an app like this and in providing constant improving updates, all in all I can't give 5 stars because if I compare it with HappyRun the latter is certainly better, especially graphically.
I always hope he improves but he will hardly reach that level. Anyway, it's a 4 star, 
I was actually thinking more of a 3 star (especially for the graphics) but I appreciate you dedicating yourself to this type of movement system and constantly trying to improve it, I appreciate your determination.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your detailed and thoughtful feedback! We’re thrilled to hear that you find our app interesting and appreciate the passion and commitment we’ve put into it.

We understand your concerns regarding the technical aspects, especially the low poly style and viewing distance. Our goal has always been to provide a smooth and enjoyable experience, and we chose the low poly style to ensure better performance on older devices and to do more story/quest content. However, we recognize the importance of enhancing the visual experience and are continuously working on optimizing the graphics while maintaining performance. 

Your suggestions are incredibly valuable to us, and we will certainly take them into consideration for future updates. We also aim to improve the fun part of the game and add more quests to enrich the environment.

We appreciate your support and understanding. Thank you for your patience and for believing in our vision to get people moving!

reviews.review_description: I hate purchasing a game just to try it out, because I've had problems getting my $ back once. But this was free to try and purchases are in the game. I like this because it's hard to find games that are compatible with lower body exercise equipment.  And this pares well with my elliptical machine. I rubber band one of the controllers to the moving handlebars.  Hang on with one hand. Hold the other controller and use the thumbsticks to control which way that I'm facing. I hope they expand this game.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your wonderful feedback! 🌟 We’re thrilled to hear that you found our game worth trying and that it pairs well with your elliptical machine. Your innovative setup with the controllers is impressive! We’re always looking to improve and expand our game, and your feedback is great! Happy gaming and exercising!

reviews.review_description: Just wondering , can you use this app on the treadmill?  I would love the option to use my treadmill and It would be great to be able to play my own music!!!

developer_response.body:
Regarding Treadmill Usage: We do not recommend using the game with a treadmill. The main concern is that players cannot see where they step, which could lead to injuries if they accidentally step outside the treadmill band. However, the game is tested with an ellipsis machine with some success.

While we don’t currently have plans for a mixed reality level specifically designed for treadmill use, it’s something we may explore in the future to enhance safety.

Regarding Custom Music: At the moment, we don’t have a solution for using your own music in the game. However, we understand that this is a highly requested feature, and we will actively explore options to address it. 

Thank you for your feedback, and we appreciate your patience as we continue to improve the game experience!

reviews.review_description: For being free this is a great find!  I use it for just walking in place since my running days are over. It reminds me a bit of the old Wii game Walk it Out minus the rhythm aspect. ( Hint hint - walking to a beat would be a fun option to add ) Collecting coins is enough of a distraction to keep the workout engaging. I would love to be able to use my own music that I have downloaded on my headset. Hoping this will be a feature soon. But in these sub zero temps right now, this game is helping me reach my daily step goal without going out of the house.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for you feedback! I will look into a way to use music to engage the player more. I love music and rhythm, so hopefully this can be a part of the game in the future :)

reviews.review_description:
Love the game, but have some suggestions (I thought I suggested multiplayer but can’t find my review)

1) Multiplayer (I think you said something about avatars being an issue)
2) If we cannot have multiplayer, please could you have a scoring system so people can challenge friends. This would really help people to get fitter by challenging each other.
3) Add records, once you complete finding all stars and diamonds, your score is updated if you beat the previous score. 
4) Add global scoring
5) Add a fitness training to track. Did you know if you star jump, you fly forward through the air (please don’t remove this). On the 1D path, I intermittently I run and then perform star jumps just to burn more calories. This could be incorporated into the 1D track (or new track) where the coach vocalises in intervals “run now” and “now star jump”, or there are marking on the track that stats this.
6) I appreciate the game is free. Not sure if this will ruin the intended style of the game, but I would love to see a slightly more realistic environment, although not overly important. 

Much appreciated,

developer_response.body:
Hey there,

Thanks a ton for your feedback! I'm so glad you're enjoying the game.

Multiplayer: At the moment, I'm not looking into multiplayer mode, but it’s definitely on the radar for the future once the basics are solid.

Scoring System: The built-in scoring system on Meta Quest already tracks your progress. You can check out the Scoreboard app on your device. Showing it directly in the game is something I'm considering for later—currently, only a few statistics are available in the info tab.

Records and Personal Bests: Tracking scores for completing all stars and diamonds is an exciting idea and is currently implemented in the Ocean Run level. More to come!

Fitness Training: Love that you're getting creative with star jumps! Adding interval vocal cues or track markings is on my (long) list of possible future additions :)

More Realistic Environments: While keeping the game free is a priority, I'll definitely consider enhancing the visual experience without straying from the intended style.

Thanks again for your support and fantastic ideas. Keep those suggestions coming!

Cheers!

reviews.review_description: This running app is an excellent way for me to stay active and have fun at the same time. I use it to close my activity rings on my Apple Watch, which is a great. The game is also very engaging, with lots of coins and diamonds to collect along the way. Overall, I would highly recommend this app to anyone looking for a fun and effective way to stay active.

developer_response.body: Great! 👍🏻

reviews.review_description: A great addition to the workout routine

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback! 
And for pushing me to finally do the music volume feature :)

reviews.review_description:
I play this nearly daily, hands down one of my everyday, favorites. Typically I'll check out the 1d level if I just want to space out and listen to a podcast using the browser.

There's a new Christmas level that was recently added, plus some other speech comments.

It's in beta, but at some point I would expect the stars to reset, or a tally of coins receive. Or a place to use the coins.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your wonderful feedback! 🌟 I’m thrilled to hear that my VR running game has become one of your everyday favorites. It’s great to know that you enjoy the 1D level while listening to podcasts—what a perfect way to unwind!

I’m excited that you noticed the new Christmas level and the added speech comments. Your suggestions about having more places to use the coins are fantastic. As of now you can use them for boost, hang glider and jetpack (in some levels). As the app is still in beta, feedback like yours is invaluable as I continue to improve the game. By the way, the 1D level is probably the next to receive some updates, or there might be a new similar level coming soon. I have yet to decide.

Thanks again for your support and have a great time running! 🏃‍♂️🎮

reviews.review_description: Thank you....An App I like...needs some work with graphics but very cool as a keep fit game...look forward to it coming to the main platform...👍🏻

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much! 
I’m working on making it look better while keeping it fast enough for both the Quest 2 and 3. 
Hopefully, it’ll be available on the main store someday. Exciting updates are on the way, so stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: This is a really good game with surprisingly good graphics. It is perfect for when you want to go out and run, but the weather is awful.

developer_response.body: That is nice to hear :)

reviews.review_description: I like the idea of this game, but sometimes it hurts my neck because of moving my head and arms to make it go. Could you add another option for movement like joystick control?

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your feedback!
Indeed, you can utilize the joystick by holding down the X-button. However, for an optimal experience, I recommend relying on your legs rather than your neck to move forward. In the app's tutorial level, there's a helpful video example that demonstrates how a gentle squat can propel you forward. Keep an eye out for the video—it's visible both in the tutorial level and behind you if you are in the main menu. Go ahead and enjoy your virtual running adventure!

reviews.review_description:
The motion is similar to alternating between the gas pedal and the brake pedal as you try to move down the path. Every stride you take ends with an abrupt stop.  The motion is quite jarring and nauseating.  

I hope the game developers can program a solution to help smooth out the motion.

developer_response.body:
* UPDATE 2! The "snow day" level is now a skiing level, where the motion is SUPER smooth. Please try it out if you got the latest version (1.6.2)

* UPDATE! We have made several improvements since you sent your feedback. Both for making the motion smoother and you can also use your arm swing to propel forward :) *

Thank you for sharing your valuable feedback. We’re committed to enhancing your experience and will certainly investigate ways to make the app’s performance smoother.

Please note, the app is designed to track your progress when it detects an up-and-down motion, similar to the natural movement during running. This is why a bouncing motion might yield the best results. We appreciate your understanding and patience as we continue to improve our app.
